data.title: Sunnova Energy International receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Sunnova Energy International (NYSE:NOVA) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing standards, which requires listed companies to maintain an average closing share price of at least $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period.
  • The company said it will closely monitor the closing share price of its common stock and is considering all available options and intends to regain compliance with the NYSE listing standards, including potentially through the consummation of a reverse stock split, subject to approval by the company’s board of directors and shareholders.
  • NOVA shares were down more than 1% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley Financial receives Nasdaq delinquency notice
data.description:
• B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) received an expected delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq that RILY was not in compliance with Nasdaq as a result of the delayed filing of the company's full year report.
  • RILY must submit a plan within 60 calendar days or June 2, 2025 to address how it intends to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq can grant an exception to up to 180 calendar days from the filing's due date, or until September 29, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • RILY shares fall 1.8% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech receives NYSE American notice of delisting and appeal
data.description:
• AIM ImmunoTech (NYSE:AIM) said on Friday that it has received notification from NYSE American that it had suspended trading of the company’s common stock and of its determination to commence delisting proceedings due to the low selling price of the company’s common stock. 
  • The company plans to appeal the determination to the exchange’s Listing Qualifications Panel. 
  • The company’s common stock is expected to begin trading on the Pink Open Market on April 7, 2025.

data.title: Chegg receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Chegg (NYSE:CHGG) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing standards because the average closing share price of the company’s common stock as of March 31, 2025, was less than $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period.
  • As required by the NYSE, the company intends to notify the NYSE timely of its intent to regain compliance with the NYSE minimum share price requirement, which may include, if necessary, effecting a reverse stock split. 
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing of the company’s common stock, which will continue to be listed and trade on the NYSE during the six-month cure period. 
  • Chegg (NYSE:CHGG) shares were up more than 3% in extended trading.

data.title: SA Asks: Which space stocks are attractive right now?
data.description:
After soaring shortly after the Trump Inauguration, space stocks have had a rough ride, with several down double-digit percentages year-to-date. Despite the turbulence, many analysts still view the sector as attractive for more adventurous investors.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Quad 7 Capital and Michael Del Monte which stocks they were favoring right now.

Quad 7 Capital: There are three space stocks we have dabbled in: Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB), Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) and AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS). We think ASTS has had the best headline news of late and is well positioned to edge higher. In our opinion, RKLB and LUNR are much more volatile and really impacted by mission-related news and contract wins.

The most near-term upside, which is what we focus on primarily, is in LUNR, in our opinion. After the IM-2 mishap, the stock reset, but the outlook for 2025 for revenue and backlog recognition is quite strong. Then we also had the tariff-induced panic slamming the market. In the medium term for LUNR, we like the growth. Any good news on missions or more contract wins will send it soaring again back to double-digits, particularly with the high short interest. We think it’s a good speculative buy in the $6s.

Michael Del Monte: I believe Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has the strongest competitive moat when considering space exploration as an industry. Companies like AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) and Globalstar (NASDAQ:GSAT) may be competing with Starlink (STRLK) in terms of direct-to-mobile satellite connectivity, making this a more crowded segment. LUNR by far has a very specific niche in developing a lunar satellite constellation with the intent of generating recurring services revenue on a per-minute basis. This is on top of the $4.82B in potential total contract value for developing the constellation.

  • Top Aerospace and Defense Stocks

data.title: SpaceX, United Launch Alliance seen winning U.S. Space Force launch contracts worth billions
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX (SPACE) and United Launch Alliance are expected to each win a U.S. Space Force rocket launch contract worth billions of dollars to send some of the Pentagon's most sensitive satellites into space over the next several years, Reuters reported Friday.

The Space Force's flagship National Security Space Launch procurement program, which is poised to assign ~50 missions through 2029 in incremental task orders, is expected to announce its selection decision on Friday, with SpaceX and ULA among the winners, according to the report.

United Launch Alliance is a joint venture between Boeing (BA) and Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) that served as the U.S. launch services provider with its Delta IV and Atlas V rockets.

data.title: S&P ends down 6%, Nasdaq in bear-market territory as Trump tariffs spurs growth scare
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday finished deeply in the red, rounding off a bloodbath of a week with Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell pointing to monetary-policy uncertainty in the face of tariff and other Trump administration fiscal policies. 

The Dow (DJI) closed in correction territory and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) pushed into bear-market territory as investors assessed sweeping new U.S. import tariffs that President Donald Trump announced this week. China, the world's second-largest economy, slapped a 34% levy on U.S. products in retaliation. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) ended -5.97% at 5,074.08, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -5.8%, to 15,587.79.  The blue-chip Dow (DJI) -5.5%, losing +2,231 points, at 38,314.86.

"Turning to monetary policy, we face a highly uncertain outlook with elevated risks of both higher unemployment and higher inflation," Powell said in a late-Friday morning speech in Virginia. "The new Administration is in the process of implementing substantial policy changes in four distinct areas: trade, immigration, fiscal policy, and regulation," Powell said.

But "the economy is still in a good place," while uncertainty is high and downside risks have risen, Powell said.

For the week, the S&P 500(SP500) tumbled 9.1%, its worst weekly decline since a -14.98% rout in the week ending March 20, 2020. It closed in a correction on Thursday. The Dow (DJI) this week dropped -7.9%, and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) slumped -10%. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) has dropped ~17% from its all-time closing high in mid-February, and the Dow (DJI) has lost ~15% from its record closing high in December. On Friday, the Energy sector (XLE) -8.7%, leading all 11 groups lower on the market benchmark (SP500). 

"The [selloff] catalyst was the tariff news but the root cause was excessively fundamentalist belief in only-up, amplified as always by too much leverage," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha. "We believe this was a selloff, not the start of a new bear market.  We think belief in the future positive may return next week”.

The bull market is "dead" as Trump's tariffs could "devastate the global economy," Bowersock Capital Partners CEO Emily Bowersock Hill said as the firm cut its 2025 S&P 500 (SP500) target to 5,700 from 6,000. 

In the bond market Friday, U.S. Treasury yields fell as traders flocked to the safety of bonds. But the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) trimmed its loss, falling 5 basis points to 4.01%, and the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) slipped 2 basis points to 3.67%.

Market participants did receive a reprieve on Friday in the form of a surprisingly strong March jobs report. Nonfarm payrolls rose 228K, blowing past expectations. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.2%. 

"Friday's jobs report shows that the economy is still creating jobs at a robust pace even with the tariff uncertainty and Federal job cuts, and job growth is arguably the most important economic indicator," Glen Smith, chief investment officer at GDS Wealth Management, said.

However, the data are "backward looking and doesn't say anything about how employers might fare over the coming months," Smith said. 

RBC Capital Markets on Friday cut its 2025 S&P 500 target by 11%, to 5,500, as it sees growth-scare risks rising amid Trump's tariff plans. 

Friday's session did see a rebound in shares of Nike (NKE), Gap (GAP), and other retailers after Vietnam and Cambodia expressed willingness to negotiate tariff levels. 

Striking an optimistic tone, Wall Street veteran Edward Yardeni told Bloomberg TV said he sees "great buying opportunities" emerging within the tariff-fueled crash in stocks.

data.title: Cipher Mining mines more bitcoins in March
data.description:
• Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) said on Friday that the crypto miner mined 210 bitcoins in March, up from 180 in February.
  • The company sold 206 bitcoins during the month, and held 1,034 BTC as of March-end.
  • Month-end operating hashrate stood at 13.5 EH/s.
  • Cipher deployed 75,000 mining rigs during the month.
